text.title: Diary Study Antipatterns and Agentic Workflow
text.summary: Common failure modes, self-report bias controls, agentic workflow guidance, and entry-coding schema for diary studies.

antipattern.description: Running analysis only after all entries are collected (batch analysis at study end).
antipattern.reason: Losing context by waiting too long: late-study entries are analyzed without the pattern visibility that incremental coding provides. Themes from early entries often reframe late-entry meaning.

antipattern.description: Signal-contingent prompts sent at fixed UTC times without per-participant timezone localization.
antipattern.reason: Prompts arriving at 2 AM local time get skipped or dashed off. LLMs default to UTC and disrespect local norms — pin to verified participant timezone table.

antipattern.description: Uploading photo entries to a cloud LLM before PII redaction.
antipattern.reason: Photo entries often contain inadvertent PII: faces, addresses, documents visible in frame. Redact before any cloud upload. EXIF metadata also contains GPS coordinates.
Quote in source language, translate at reporting time only. Auto-translation during analysis changes nuance and destroys the verbatim evidence needed for credibility.

rule.statement: Treat participant self-report as directional signal, not metric. Never aggregate sentiment scores from diary entries into a single KPI without human coding validation.
rule.rationale: Self-report bias suppresses negative and embarrassing moments. Participants under-report breakdowns in contexts where they feel observed. Sentiment scoring of self-report is noisy; LLM synthesis can generate patterns absent in sparse longitudinal data.

section.title: Agentic Workflow
section.p: Agents are strong for: scaffolding (study plan, screener, entry templates, reminder cadence), automating follow-up scheduling, clustering and theme extraction from the entry corpus, attrition tracking. Agents are weak for: generating synthetic insights before human-coded baseline (LLMs over-fit to their own clustering), speaker attribution in audio entries, PII detection in images.
Schedule reminder dispatch via cron or queue, not conversational chat. Diary studies live or die on logistics consistency — an agent that misses a reminder cohort destroys that day's data window.
Entry coding schema for LLM-assisted analysis:

example.code: { "timestamp": "ISO8601", "location_context": "home|work|commute|other", "trigger": "string or null", "outcome": "string or null", "sentiment": -2, "feature_used": ["string"], "breakdown_reported": false, "quote": "verbatim or null" }
example.note: Mark uncertain fields as null. Never invent quote text. Output as JSONL, one entry per line.

section.reference: Script: emit per-participant reminder schedule with early-study weighting
